MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-15-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES: Atty. YOSEF PERETZ, ESQ. of Peretz & Associates (Tel: 415-732-3777) appearing for plaintiff Maria Isabel Delgado;
Atty. ROBIN JAMES, ESQ. of Michelman & Robinson, LLP (Tel: 415-882-7770) appearing for defendants Wells Fargo & Co. and Wells Fargo Bank, N.A.

Matter is argued and submitted by counsel for both sides. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. Defendant Wells Fargo Insurance, Inc.'s motion to strike the amendment to the complaint filed October 29, 2018 or, in the alternative, to strike specified portions of the complaint is denied in its entirety. WFI's request for judicial notice of portions of plaintiff Maria Delgado's deposition testimony is denied. While the parties appear to view the judicial notice issue as either the content of all deposition testimony of a party that is inconsistent with the party's pleading is judicially noticeable or the content of a party's deposition testimony is never judicially noticeable, this court adopts the more nuanced and better-reasoned "third approach" set forth in Joslin v. H.A.S. Insurance Brokerage (1986) 184 Cal. App. 369, 375 that deposition testimony of a pleader is judicially noticeable only "to the extent that [the statements in the deposition] were not or could not be disputed." Because Ms. Delgado vigorously disputes that she verified the discovery responses that were served in October 2018 and WFI initially received consent for the purchase of the National Union insurance, application of third approach requires denial of WFI's request for judicial notice. The denial of WFI's request for judicial notice also requires the denial of the motion. Ms. Delgado's request for sanctions per CCP 128.5 is denied because WFI's motion had some arguable legal and factual support and thus was not frivolous and there is no basis to conclude that the motion was filed solely for the purposes of delay when the motion did not and could not delay the pre-existing trial date. FORMAL ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT.
